key_drivers: Real yields now FLAT at 1.87% (DFII10, ±5bps from prior report) — the dominant bullish driver from the 1930 EST report has dissipated; this is the primary reason for lower equity scores vs prior session

Iran war/Strait of Hormuz blockade continues: IEA projects 8 mb/d supply disruption; API crude inventory BUILD of +6.56M barrels for week ending March 14 creates short-term bearish counterpoint for CL

Pre-FOMC Day 2 environment (decision today March 18-19): Fed universally expected to hold at 3.50-3.75% (98.9% per CME FedWatch); gold and commodities retain geopolitical safe-haven bid; equities await Fed statement language on oil/inflation path

stale_sources: FRED DFII10 (1-day lag, March 16 data — next release March 18)

MOVE from TradingView TVC:MOVE (79.23, -7.06% on March 17)
data_quality.notes: Report generated at 0242 EST on March 18, 2026 — overnight pre-FOMC session. Real yields (DFII10) remain at 1.87% as of March 16 (1-day stale), scored as FLAT (±5bps). VIX direction and MOVE direction scored as FLAT for overnight session (intraday direction from March 17 close no longer actionable). API crude inventory data (+6.56M barrel build for week ending March 14) sourced from Reuters/CNBC news reports. RBA hike to 4.10% confirmed March 17. BoJ and ECB decisions both expected March 18-19 — pre-meeting uncertainty reduces confidence by 1 point on rate-sensitive instruments (ES, NQ, YM, RTY, M6E, 6J). EIA weekly petroleum report due today (March 18) — next data update will incorporate official EIA inventory figures.
